message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: Adding opt-in disk compression (magic-header pickle) + adaptive eviction knobs (cull_limit, eviction_policy) to Cache class and configure_cache(). What are you working on?

type: PROPOSE
my_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: Implementing TTL support for cache layers (memory+disk). Need to edit both files. What is your feature and does it touch these files?

type: REVISE
my_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
your_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: Same 2 files needed by both features, can't be fully disjoint. Propose functional split instead: I own compression logic only (compress param in Cache.__init__, _encode/_decode helpers w/ DSPC magic header, put/get compression branches, configure_cache's new compress/cull_limit/eviction_policy params). You own TTL logic only (ttl params in Cache.__init__, expiry checks, configure_cache's new ttl params). We each add our own new params to the existing signatures without touching each other's lines, commit separately, then merge via git at the end. I'll git merge team/agent1 before writing patch.txt. Sound good?

type: REVISE
my_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
your_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: Our features both fundamentally require touching cache.py + __init__.py (TTL vs compression/eviction), so a disjoint split isn't possible. Let's both own both files but follow conventions to ease the merge: (1) add new constructor/configure_cache params at the END of the existing parameter list, never reorder existing ones; (2) keep changes localized to lines relevant to your own feature, don't reformat/touch unrelated code; (3) in Cache.get()/put() only add new branches, don't restructure existing control flow. I'll handle merge conflict resolution manually at the end via git merge. OK with this?

type: ACCEPT
my_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
your_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: Agreed: new params appended at end of signatures, changes localized to our own feature's lines, only additive branches in get/put. Starting implementation now.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
your_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: Agreed: functional split, I own TTL logic only (ttl param + set_ttl helper), you own compression/eviction logic only. Each adds own params to end of signatures, no touching each other's lines. Proceeding to implement now.

type: DONE
my_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: TTL implementation done: Cache(ttl=...), configure_cache(ttl=...), set_ttl(seconds) exported from dspy.clients, TTLCache swap in memory layer, disk_cache.set(expire=ttl) + lazy expire() purge on miss. All existing tests pass. Ready to merge whenever you are.

type: DONE
my_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: Compression + eviction-knob implementation complete and pushed to team/agent2 (commit 0211e4d5). Tests pass (15/15 in tests/clients/test_cache.py + tests/caching/test_litellm_cache.py). Ready to merge your TTL work whenever you're done.
